content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA. The diversity of the array AAA is defined as number of pairs i,j,i<ji, j, i < j i,j,i<j such that Ai‚âÝAjA_i \neq A_jAi‚ÄãÓÄÝ=Aj‚Äã.

You want to maximize the diversity of the array. For that, you are allowed to make at most KKK operations on it, in any of which, you can select a particular element and change its value to any integer in the range 1 to 10910^9109, both inclusive.

Find out the maximum diversity of the array that you can obtain.

###Input:


First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
The first line of each testcase contains two integers N,KN, KN,K. where NNN denotes the length of array AAA.
The next line of each testcase contains NNN space separated integers, the i-th of which denotes AiA_iAi‚Äã.
###Output:
For each testcase, output in a single line, the answer corresponding to the testcase, which should be an integer denoting the maximum possible diversity.

###Constraints


1‚â§T‚â§201 \leq T \leq 201‚â§T‚â§20
0‚â§K‚â§1090 \leq K \leq 10^90‚â§K‚â§109
2‚â§N‚â§1052 \leq N \leq 10^52‚â§N‚â§105
1‚â§Ai‚â§1091 \leq A_i \leq 10^91‚â§Ai‚Äã‚â§109
